---
title: Introduction to Health Data Analytics and R
description: |
  **Delivery Mode:** Online (self-paced independent learning)
  
  **Start date:** Anytime
  
  **Cost:** Free
  
  **Duration:** 2 weeks
site-url: https://shortcourses.ed.ac.uk/course/introduction-to-health-data-analytics-and-r
image: medical-banner-with-doctor-wearing-goggles.jpg
#image-alt: |
categories: []
course-format: [PG, UG, short-course]
open-graph: true
twitter-card: true
# will inherit title, description, image
# to change ANYTHING about card, need to fill in EVERYTHING below
#  title: |
#  description: |
#  image: |
#  image-alt: |
#  defaults to 500 x 500 summary: uncomment lines below for large card
#  image-width: 600 
#  image-height: 314
#  card-style: summary_large_image 
#  image-width: 500
#  image-height: 500
#  card-style: summary
draft: false
---
::: {.profile-image-float-left}
![](medical-banner-with-doctor-wearing-goggles.jpg){fig-align="centre"}
:::

## About the course

In this course, we will discuss what we mean by Health Data Analytics and then introduce you to a practical way you can begin to work with health data analytics by using one of the most widely used programming languages in the world of data science: R. R can be used across a wide range of health data applications from AI to bioinformatics to administration and epidemiology.  

For more information and enrollment options, please click [here](https://shortcourses.ed.ac.uk/course/introduction-to-health-data-analytics-and-r)

*Course [image](https://www.freepik.com/free-photo/medical-banner-with-doctor-wearing-goggles_30555909.htm) by Freepik, 2025, free to use of Freepick*
